---
summary: 'Bundled, pre-built apps you clone-and-deploy from the desktop gallery.'
title: 'Templates'
---

# Templates

Templates are pre-built Centraid apps that ship with the desktop. Each one is **identical in shape** to an app you'd author yourself вЂ” `index.html`, `app.css`, `app.js`, `queries/`, `actions/`, `migrations/`, `app.json`. You clone, you tweak, you ship.

The templates live in [`packages/app-blueprints/`](https://github.com/srikanthsrungarapu/centraid/tree/main/packages/app-blueprints).

## The two flavors

### Plain apps

Single-purpose UIs. Cup tracker. Todos. Daily journal. These are the seed shape for "an app that you open and click a button on".

| Template  | What it is                                                                 |
| --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `hydrate` | Track 8 cups a day. One table, one query, one action. The reference shape. |
| `todos`   | Capture and clear small things.                                            |
| `journal` | A clean place to write each day.                                           |

### Automation-flavored

Templates with `kind: "automation"` in the gallery's `index.json` (separate from each app's own `app.json` вЂ” see the distinction below). These are headless automation apps: an `app.json` with empty `actions[]` / `queries[]`, no `index.html`, and one or more automations under `automations/<id>/`. The handler (`handler.js`) typically gathers data via `ctx.tool`, calls `ctx.agent` to reason, and writes the result back. Designed for "the agent does the work in the background; you read the result". See the [Automations](/automations/index) tab for the full runtime.

| Template                       | Category     | Trigger            |
| ------------------------------ | ------------ | ------------------ |
| `auto.briefing`                | Daily rhythm | Weekdays В· 6:00 PM |
| `auto.evening-wrap-up`         | Daily rhythm | Daily В· 9:00 PM    |
| `auto.meeting-prep`            | Daily rhythm | (see template)     |
| `auto.email-triage`            | Inbox        | (see template)     |
| `auto.issue-triage`            | Inbox        | (see template)     |
| `auto.pr-review-digest`        | Engineering  | (see template)     |
| `auto.release-notes-drafter`   | Engineering  | (see template)     |
| `auto.flaky-test-tracker`      | Engineering  | (see template)     |
| `auto.dependency-update-check` | Engineering  | (see template)     |
| `auto.system-health-check`     | Engineering  | (see template)     |

> **TODO(#120)** вЂ” flesh out the per-template trigger schedules. They're declared in each template's `index.json` entry as `triggerLabel`; worth pulling them all into this table.

## What's inside `packages/app-blueprints/`

```
packages/app-blueprints/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.md
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ src/                      # listTemplates(), types
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ index.json                # the manifest source вЂ” TemplateManifest
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ manifest.json             # generated by scripts/build-manifest.mjs
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ hydrate/                  # one folder per template, full app shape
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ todos/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ journal/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auto.briefing/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auto.evening-wrap-up/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ вЂ¦
```

Each `<id>/` directory is a fully-formed app. The `index.json` at the top is the **gallery manifest** вЂ” what the desktop reads to render the template picker.

### Gallery manifest entry

For a plain app:

```json
{
  "id": "hydrate",
  "name": "Hydrate",
  "desc": "Track 8 cups a day.",
  "colorKey": "indigo",
  "iconKey": "Water",
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

For an automation:

```json
{
  "id": "auto.briefing",
  "name": "Briefing",
  "desc": "A scannable catch-up вЂ¦",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "kind": "automation",
  "emoji": "рџЊ¤",
  "category": "Daily rhythm",
  "triggerKind": "cron",
  "triggerLabel": "Weekdays В· 6:00 PM",
  "integrations": ["Google Calendar", "Gmail", "Slack"]
}
```

The `kind: "automation"` flag plus `triggerKind` / `triggerLabel` / `integrations` are what the gallery uses to render automations differently from regular apps.

This `kind` field is **gallery-level metadata only** вЂ” it lives on the template-gallery entry in `packages/app-blueprints/index.json`, not on the runtime `app.json` inside the template directory. The runtime doesn't read it. The desktop's gallery picker reads it to decide which tab to surface the template under (`Apps` vs `Automations`).

## Pre-built handlers

Templates ship with **compiled `.js` already in place**. Cloning is "click and deploy" вЂ” no `bun install`, no compile step.

> Pre-build the handlers: commit the compiled `.js` files alongside `.ts` so cloning is "click and deploy" вЂ” no `bun install` needed on first use.
> вЂ” [`packages/app-blueprints/README.md`](https://github.com/srikanthsrungarapu/centraid/blob/main/packages/app-blueprints/README.md)

This is what makes the gallery feel instant.
